RULING - The matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PARHAM BARGHCHI , AN INDIVIDUAL, AND IN HIS REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES' Motion Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action.

Grant. No opposition filed. Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kaitlin Martinez, Esq. 415-580-0997 Emran Law Firm for Plaintiff.
John Jung, Esq. 415-926-7646 Fisher & Phillips for Defendant.

ARGUED. Plaintiff's counsel Ms. Martinez contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. Defense' counsel Mr. Junq contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF PARHAM BARGHCHI's Motion Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Parham Barghchi's unopposed motion requesting dismissal of entire action is DENIED. Plaintiff moves under CCP section 581(b)(1) and Labor Code section 2699(l). Plaintiff seeks to dismiss individual and representative PAGA claims. The court construes Plaintiff's reference to Labor Code section 2699(l) (which itself is inapplicable to this motion) as a reference to section 2699(s), which, in fact, applies to motions to settle PAGA claims. "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part." (Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2).) Here, Plaintiff effectively seeks judicial approval of a settlement of his individual and representative PAGA claims, but he has not provided a copy of the settlement for the court's review. The court will not approve a settlement it has not reviewed. Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that he submitted the proposed settlement to the LWDA. The court, thus, cannot approve the settlement and dismissal under section 2699(s)(2). Nor can or will the court approve dismissal under section 581(b)(1), given that the dismissal is based on settlement of the PAGA claims, which cannot be approved on this record.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
